items.title: Tehran and Washington sign interim peace deal to end US-Israeli war on Iran
items.description: President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed an interim deal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran. New US Fed chair vows reforms as central bank signals rate hikes on horizon
items.description: As widely expected, policymakers at the the US Federal Reserve have kept interest rates unchanged. But in a clear change of tone from previous meetings, Fed officials said they expected raising interest rates later this year rather than lowering them, to tame rising inflation. The meeting was the first chaired by the central bank's new chief, Kevin Warsh, who scrapped guidance about future rate moves and announced new task forces that could reshape how the Fed communicates and analyses data.

items.description: Chase died from sepsis following a bout with meningitis in a Los Angeles hospital, her longtime manager told the BBC.

items.title: Iran pledges no bomb; U.S. promises sanctions relief, $300 billion financing; agreement’s leaked text shows
items.description: Tehran had promised never to make a nuclear bomb in 2015 Obama deal, which Mr. Trump pulled out of; draft calls for end to war on all fronts, including Lebanon; Strait of Hormuz to reopen fully within 30 days

items.title: Lula says Trump 'should not meddle' in Brazil's upcoming elections
items.description: Brazilian president says it's fine for Donald Trump to support the right-wing Bolsonaro family, but that he "should not meddle" in the nation's October presidential election. Leer más

items.title: 'Took my breath away': Afghanistan gallery opening soon at Australian War Memorial
items.description: Australian War Memorial director Matt Anderson is asked about an invitation issued to Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been accused of war crimes, to the upcoming opening of a new gallery space dedicated to Australia's service in Afghanistan.

items.title: EU lawmakers approve deportation hubs for migrants
items.description: European lawmakers gave final approval Wednesday to tougher migration rules that will grant authorities much broader detention powers and allow for the creation of deportation centres outside the bloc

items.title: Bozize trial over alleged war crimes opens in Bangui
items.description: Former president Francois Bozize may not be in the courtroom, but three of his former senior military officers were present as his landmark trial got underway in Bangui.

items.title: Bruce Lehrmann makes legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage
items.description: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has asked a court to order the company behind a film involving Brittany Higgins to hand over raw footage.

items.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
items.description: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

items.title: What is Google’s Project Nimbus, and why are students protesting against it?
items.description: Amazon and Google’s Project Nimbus cloud contract with Israel has sparked multiple protests over U.S. Big Tech enabling the Israeli military’s violence against Palestinians

items.title: Ukraine hits Moscow oil refinery, disrupting commercial flights in major drone attack
items.description: The attack comes as President Vladimir Putin is in Kazan, 700 km east of Moscow, hosting leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations as Russia seeks to bolster business and other ties

items.title: An inside look at President Trump's campaign to acquire Greenland
items.description: New Yorker writer Ben Taub says while the idea of acquiring Greenland is out of the headlines, it hasn't been dropped. Taub describes how Trump's ongoing efforts have broken the trust of our allies.

items.title: Argentina launch bid for back-to-back title in Messi's sixth World Cup
items.description: Algeria will be Argentina's first hurdle in Group J, while Austria and Jordan await in Dallas on June 22 and June 27. Leer más

items.title: Trump says he will visit India as frosty relationship with Modi thaws
items.description: Trump made the announcement during chat where he also vowed to protect India.

items.title: Arruabarrena set to return to Boca Juniors after talks with Riquelme
items.description: Former coach says he is 'excited' by prospect of a second spell at La Bombonera, a decade after leading the club to a league-and-cup double. Leer más

items.title: Government of Equatorial Guinea submits collective resignation
items.description: The government of Equatorial Guinea has resigned after failing to meet its targets.

items.title: Messi ties World Cup goals record as Argentina tops Algeria
items.description: Remarkably, the hat trick came exactly 20 years after Messi made his World Cup debut. It was also his fifth consecutive World Cup match with a goal, further strengthening his claim as football’s greatest player.

items.title: Trump’s MoU with Iran draws backlash from some Republicans
items.description: Louisiana Senator Bill Cassidy blasts US-Iran MoU as 'worst foreign policy blunder in decades'.

items.title: Donald Trump and his Ukrainian bête noire
items.description: Hard as it is for Trump to stomach, Zelenskyy has greatly distinguished himself as a war leader. As for Trump, unless he is very lucky, he could find his name permanently attached to a deal with the Iranians that will bring to mind Neville Chamberlain. Leer más

items.title: Tireless human rights activist Taty Almeida, founding member of Madres de Plaza de Mayo, dies at 95
items.description: Teacher, iconic human rights activist, president of Madres de Plaza de Mayo Línea Fundadora and campaigner for the disappeared and downtrodden. Taty Almeida, who has died at the age of 95, would not be silenced in her tireless search for memory, truth and justice and the knowledge over what happened to her missing son Alejandro. Leer más

items.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
items.description: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
items.description: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

items.title: Court warns former president Cristina Fernández de Kirchner over supporters' rallies outside home
items.description: Court warns former president over supporters' rallies outside her home and says she must comply with conditions of house arrest or face seeing restrictions tightened. Leer más

items.title: UK, Finland embassies speak up for freedom of press
items.description: UK Ambassador to Argentina David Cairns and Finland's Ambassador to Argentina Nicola Lindertz celebrate Journalists' Day and speak up in defence of "a free, independent and pluralistic press." Leer más

items.title: Trump welcomed to Versailles for dinner with Macron
items.description: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

items.title: Germany news: Industrial employment falls to decade low
items.description: A new study shows the decline was due to weaker hiring activity rather than redundancies. And at the World Cup, Germany may be taking a break but German coaches are in the thick of the action. DW has more.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence and natural stupidity
items.description: Capitulation to robots is always going to be a hard sell. Leer más

items.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
items.description: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

items.title: Argentina races ahead of storm in first training session with Marcos Senesi
items.description: Defender Marcos Senesi joins the squad in Kansas City as Lionel Scaloni finally has his full group for Argentina's World Cup title defence in the tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Leer más

items.title: African and Commonwealth nations in Kenya urge quick execution of key treaty protecting oceans
items.description: African and Commonwealth nations in Kenya urge quick execution of key treaty protecting oceans

items.title: Trump says it’s ‘unfair’ for Iran to lack ballistic missiles
items.description: President Donald Trump says it’s ‘unfair’ for Iran to lack ballistic missiles if other regional countries have them.

items.title: South Africa coach tells critics to ‘shut up’ before second World Cup match
items.description: 'I never listen to the ​trash of the social media,' Hugo Broos says before Bafana Bafana's match against Czechia.

items.title: EU, G7 pledge support amid DRC Ebola outbreak
items.description: G7 leaders called for a "strong and coordinated response" to the latest Ebola outbreak. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen said that "health security is shared security." The recorded death toll in DRC is approaching 200.

items.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
items.description: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

items.title: Rwanda stepping up precautions against Ebola, health minister says
items.description: “The spread of this current outbreak came as a surprise,” says Rwandan health minister Sabin Nsanzimana about the current Ebola epidemic in the DRC. Since the outbreak was declared on May 15, 808 cases have been confirmed in the DRC, including 192 deaths, according to the World Health Organization.

items.title: Africa urgently needs more fish farms, UN tells ocean conference
items.description: Africa needs to urgently expand its fish-farming sector to meet its food needs, the head of the UN's fisheries division said Tuesday, even as its latest report found record production levels globally.

items.title: Eviction notice for elderly Melbourne renter ruled invalid
items.description: HousingFirst has lost its bid to repossess the inner-east Melbourne property of an elderly tenant who disputed the validity of a rental increase.

items.title: Bitcoin specialists cast doubt on Adorni's account of wealth surge
items.description: Blockchain specialist and programmer analyse Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni’s Bitcoin explanation, which he says is responsible for the growth in his assets and wealth; They conclude the facts don’t add up. Leer más

items.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
items.description: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

items.title: Sudan’s young women return to international soccer as war and taboos linger
items.description: Their red jerseys stood out against the green pitch. Most were teenage girls. Some had fled war. Others had never played in an organized soccer league or set foot in a major stadium before.

items.title: Veteran Congolese doctor still helping fight Ebola at 84
items.description: In 1976, young Congolese doctor Jean-Jacques Muyembe was sent to a remote village to look into a mysterious illness and found himself confronting Ebola, a deadly virus the world did not yet know.

items.title: Iran's hardliners fear being sidelined in US deal
items.description: As an Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war moves closer to formal signature, hardline supporters of the Islamic Republic fear losing influence as Iran's politics shift from maximalist defiance to compromise.

items.title: Mining threatens scarce water resources in the Andes
items.description: San Juan Province is facing a persistent water shortage due to climate change, while mining advances across the Andes and its thousands of glaciers. Leer más

items.title: Mali: Mandatory motorcycle registration raises concerns among stakeholders
items.description: In Bamako, motorcycles are impossible to ignore. They are everywhere — in narrow streets, on major roads, at the heart of daily life. For tens of thousands of families, riding a two-wheeler is simply a means of survival.

items.title: US transfers immigrants out of Florida Alligator Alcatraz detention centre
items.description: The facility, located near the Everglade wetlands, has drawn human rights concerns and lawsuits over its conditions.

items.title: Exclusive-UK in advanced talks with Nissan over Sunderland plant support, sources say
